name: Template Alarm Control Panel
description: The `template` alarm control panel platform allows you to turn your binary sensors into a state machine managed alarm control panel.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name of the alarm control panel. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the number in the frontend.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Arming Away Time
config_entries.description: The exit delay before the alarm is armed to away mode. Defaults to `0s`.

config_entries.label: Arming Home Time
config_entries.description: The exit delay before the alarm is armed to home mode.

config_entries.label: Arming Night Time
config_entries.description: The exit delay before the alarm is armed to night mode.

config_entries.label: Binary Sensors
config_entries.description: ***list***: A list of binary sensors the panel should use. Each consists of:

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: The id of the binary sensor component

config_entries.label: Bypass Armed Home
config_entries.description: This binary sensor will not trigger the alarm when in `armed_home` state.

config_entries.label: Bypass Armed Night
config_entries.description: This binary sensor will not trigger the alarm when in `armed_night` state.

config_entries.label: Bypass Auto
config_entries.description: This binary sensor will be automatically bypassed if left on/open at the time of arming.

config_entries.label: Chime
config_entries.description: When set `true`, the chime callback will be called whenever the sensor goes from closed to open. (`false` is the default if not specified)

config_entries.label: Trigger Mode
config_entries.description: Sets the trigger mode for this sensor. One of `delayed`, `instant`, `instant_always`, or `delayed_follower`. (`delayed` is the default if not specified)

config_entries.label: Codes
config_entries.description: A list of codes for disarming the alarm, if *requires_code_to_arm* set to true then for arming the alarm too.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Pending Time
config_entries.description: The entry delay before the alarm is triggered. Defaults to `0s`.

config_entries.label: Requires Code To Arm
config_entries.description: Code required for arming the alarm, *codes* must be provided.

config_entries.label: Trigger Time
config_entries.description: The time after a triggered alarm before resetting to previous state if the sensors are cleared/off. Defaults to `0s`.
